purpose: Reference outputs and scoring rubrics for three real workflow construction cases. These are not execution prompts; they are quality gates for judging workflow outputs against a strong-model reference.

case_quantum_funding_h1_2025.task: Search H1 2025 global quantum-computing startup funding rounds and amounts; output grouped tables by round, country, and application direction plus competitive impact summary.

reference_output.source_policy: Use announced financing date in 2025-01-01..2025-06-30; exclude later extensions, IPOs, public-market financings, acquisitions, and window-out events.

core_events.application_direction: fault-tolerant quantum computers; cat qubits/error-resistant architecture
core_events.investors_or_notes: Led by Future French Champions, AXA Venture Partners, and Bpifrance per company/HPCwire coverage.
Company says capital accelerates development and production of large-scale fault-tolerant systems.

core_events.application_direction: quantum control systems and hybrid quantum-classical infrastructure
core_events.investors_or_notes: Company reports total funding reached USD 280M; technology used across multiple quantum-computer types.

core_events.application_direction: superconducting QPU architecture and modular processors
core_events.investors_or_notes: Company says round funds scaling of VIO platform and powerful quantum processors.

grouping_expectations.by_round: Series A: QuantWare; Series B/large financing: Alice & Bob, QuEra; Series C: Quantum Machines.
grouping_expectations.by_country: United States: QuEra; France: Alice & Bob; Israel: Quantum Machines; Netherlands: QuantWare.
grouping_expectations.by_application: Fault-tolerant hardware: Alice & Bob, QuEra; quantum control/infrastructure: Quantum Machines; QPU processor architecture: QuantWare.

reference_output.competitive_impact_summary: The H1 2025 funding signal is concentrated around fault-tolerant hardware and enabling infrastructure rather than near-term application software. QuEra and Alice & Bob indicate capital moving toward distinct fault-tolerant hardware bets, while Quantum Machines shows that control-stack vendors can attract late-stage capital because they sell into many hardware modalities. QuantWare adds a modular superconducting QPU supply-chain angle. A strong answer should avoid claiming a complete market census unless it has broader database coverage, and should label this as a source-backed sample of notable public rounds.

sources.title: Quantum Machines Raises $170 million in Series C funding
Alice & Bob Closes EUR 100M Series B
QuantWare raises EUR 20 million Series A

criteria: Only includes financing events announced in 2025H1.
Every amount and round has a source URL or clear locator.
Tables are grouped by round, country/region, and application direction.
Competition summary separates fact, inference, and uncertainty.
Does not present the reference list as exhaustive unless broader funding databases are cited.
fail_conditions: Includes H2 2025, 2026, IPO, public-market, or acquisition events as H1 startup funding.
Invents amounts, lead investors, countries, or application directions.
Omits source status or confidence for financing rows.

case_ai_ide_pricing_compare.task: Search latest Cursor, GitHub Copilot, and Windsurf/Devin Desktop pricing and features; output difference matrix plus selection advice.

reference_output.source_policy: Prefer official pricing/docs pages. Third-party pricing explainers are allowed only to flag disputed or recently changed billing mechanics.

matrix_core.individual_paid: Pro around USD 20/month; higher tiers such as Pro+/Ultra are usage-heavy tiers where available
matrix_core.team_plan: Teams shown at USD 40/user/month on official pricing page
matrix_core.key_features: Agentic editor, cloud agents/automations, team billing/admin, team privacy mode, SAML/OIDC SSO on teams per official page snippets
matrix_core.risk_notes: Usage/credit details change frequently; output must include retrieval date and quote source status.

matrix_core.entry_plan: Free plan exists; individual paid tiers include Pro/Pro+/Max in current plan taxonomy
matrix_core.organization_paid: Business and Enterprise plans; official docs emphasize centralized management, policy control, broad model catalog, cloud agent, and monthly AI credit pool
matrix_core.key_features: Deep GitHub integration, code completion, chat, cloud agent, organization policy/license management, IP indemnity for org offerings
matrix_core.risk_notes: Usage-based billing and AI credits changed in 2026; current output must distinguish subscription fee from extra usage exposure.

matrix_core.individual_paid: Pro USD 20/month and Max USD 200/month shown on Devin pricing page
matrix_core.team_plan: Team plan shown at USD 80/month plus full-user pricing on Devin pricing page; older Windsurf Teams naming may appear in older docs
matrix_core.key_features: Devin Desktop/Tab, premium models, fast context, Devin Cloud/DeepWiki/API/Ask Devin depending on plan
matrix_core.risk_notes: Must not confuse old Windsurf credit plans with post-2026 Devin Desktop plans; name migration should be called out.

selection_advice.solo_lightweight: Cursor Pro or Copilot Pro depending on whether editor-native agent workflows or GitHub-native assistance matters more.
selection_advice.heavy_agent_coding: Cursor higher usage tiers or Devin Desktop Max/Team are stronger candidates; require budget caps and usage monitoring.
selection_advice.github_org: Copilot Business/Enterprise is preferred when GitHub policy management, license control, and org integration dominate.
selection_advice.budget_sensitive_team: Start with official team entry prices and enforce spend controls; avoid tools with unclear overage exposure until billing mechanics are verified.
selection_advice.centralized_enterprise: Copilot Enterprise or Cursor Teams/Enterprise depending on GitHub governance vs editor-agent workflow; Devin Enterprise if cloud agent/desktop workflows are the main operating model.

sources.title: Introducing our new Windsurf pricing plans

criteria: Uses official product pages as primary evidence.
Includes retrieval date and source status for all prices.
Separates individual, heavy-user, team, and enterprise plans.
Explains usage/credit/quota risk rather than only monthly headline prices.
Selection advice is scenario-based and tied to matrix evidence.
fail_conditions: Uses stale third-party pricing as final truth when official pages differ.
Confuses Windsurf legacy naming with Devin Desktop current pricing.
Gives generic advice without price/use-case evidence.

case_tesla_fsd_v13_social_alert.task: Monitor the past 48 hours of Reddit/Twitter/X discussion about Tesla FSD V13; detect major accidents or regulatory warnings; trigger high-level alert if hit.

reference_output.time_window: 2026-06-30 to 2026-07-02, interpreted relative to current run date.
reference_output.source_policy: Only in-window Reddit/Twitter/X posts or in-window news/regulatory sources can trigger alert. Window-out Reddit posts about FSD 13.x crashes are context only. If X/Twitter is inaccessible, source_status must be missing and cannot be treated as evidence.

observed_context.item: Business Insider article published today about missing Tesla Autopilot fatal crash data
observed_context.alert_use: context only; do not count as FSD V13 social hit

observed_context.item: Wired/People reports about a Texas fatal crash and lawsuit published around 6 days earlier
observed_context.claim_type: fatal crash / FSD or Autopilot allegations
observed_context.alert_use: context only for monitoring background; cannot trigger 48-hour V13 alert

observed_context.item: Reddit posts about FSD 13.2.x crashes or near misses found in public search
observed_context.window_status: mostly months to over a year old
observed_context.alert_use: must not trigger current high-level alert

expected_alert_result.severity: P3 no current high-level alert, or P2 watch if the workflow includes in-window non-V13 regulatory/legal context
expected_alert_result.reason: Accessible sources did not establish a credible in-window Reddit/Twitter/X FSD V13 major accident or regulatory warning. Current/high-relevance but non-V13 or out-of-window evidence should be preserved as context, not escalated.
expected_alert_result.required_caveat: If direct Twitter/X API access is unavailable, the output must explicitly state coverage gap and recommend API-backed rerun.

sources.title: The case of the missing Tesla fatal crash data
A Fatal Tesla Crash in Texas Sets Up a Legal Showdown
Tesla Full Self-Driving Vehicle Safety Report

criteria: Every social/news item has posted_at/published_at and window_status.
Clearly states Reddit and Twitter/X coverage status.
Does not trigger P0/P1 without in-window credible accident or regulatory evidence.
Preserves context from older or non-V13 incidents without treating it as current alert evidence.
Provides next monitoring queries and platform/API coverage gaps.
fail_conditions: Triggers high-level alert from old Reddit posts or 6-day-old news.
Claims Twitter/X monitoring succeeded without accessible source links or API results.
Treats general Tesla Autopilot/FSD legal news as FSD V13-specific evidence without support.
